content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
JuIy 12, 1990
John Honigsfeld
35O S. Fuller Ave. #2B
Los Angeles, CA 90036
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-406
Dear Mr. Honigsfeld:
You frave reguested advice on behalf of severaL Peace and
Freedom Party candidates for Controller, Treasurer, secretary
State, Board of Equalization, and various Assembly and State
Senate races. Your question concerns the campaign provisions
the Political 
Reform Act. r
OUESTIONS
1. How do Forms 47O, 5O1, and 502 relate to each other?
2. If a candidate receives a $25 contribution and does not
have a bank account, how can the candidate conply with the Act if
the candidate can not find a bank willing to open a checking
account for 52 5?
CONCLUSIONS
1. There is no rrrelationshipt! between the Forn 470 and the
Forns 5O1 and 502, per se. The requirenent to file the Form 470
is not contingent upon rrhether the Forms 5O1 or 502 are filed.
Conversely, the Forns 501 and 502 have their own separate filing
requirernents .
The Form 470 (candidate and officeholder Carnpaign statement -
- Short Forn) may be filed by a candidate in connection srith a
statewide, an Assembly, or a State Senate election in lieu of Form
490 (candidate and officeholder Campaign Statenent -- Long Forn)
if the candidate does not intend to raise or spend $1,000 in a
calendar year for political 
purposes, i.ncluding the candidate's
own personal funds. The Form 470 is filed even if the candidate
does not raise or spend any money.
1 
co.r".rrnent code Sections 81OOO-91015. AIl statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Cornmission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AIl references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
Of
Of
428 J Strect, Suitc St'to o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacranrenro C.{ 95tirr.+-(rstr7 o (91(>) l2l-i66O


John Honigsfeld
Page Two
The Form 5O1 (Candidate Intention) must be filed prior to a
candidate soliciting or accepting caupaign contributions,
including the use of the candidate's personal funds for political
purposes. The only exception is when the candidate does not
intend to receive contributions or make expenditures to support
his or her candidacy and uses personal funds onl,y to pay for
filing fees and candidate qualification statement fees. In this
case, the candidate is not required to file the ForD 501.
However, if canpaign contributions are received, or the candidate
uses personal funds to make other types of expenditures to support
his or her candidacy, the funds nust be placed into a bank account
with a financial institution located in California.
The Form 502 (Canpaign Bank Account) is f i.Ied within 24 hours
after opening the bank account.
2. The Act does not specify the tlT)e of bank account into
which contributions must be placed. Therefore, as an example, the
$25 contribution could be placed into a savings account and
expenditures from the account nade by noney order. In addition,
the candidate night choose to loan his or her canpaign the funds
necessary to open a bank account so that if contributions are
received, an account exists into which they could be placed. In
the alternative, the candidate could return the contribution to
the contributor. 
Under no circumstances, however, may a
candidate place canpaign contributions received into a personal
bank account cornningled with personal funds.
FACTS
You represent Peace and Freedom candidates running for
Controller, Treasurer, secretary of State, Board of Equalization,
and various state Senate and Assembly seats. None of the
candidates listed in your letter has raised or spent any money for
political 
purposes and, therefore, has not filed either the Forn
501 or 502. Since no candidate expects to raise or spend 91,OOO
this calendar year, aLl of the candidates have filed the Forn 470.
ANALYSIS
Sections 842OO, 84200.5, 842OO.6, A42OO.7, 84200.8 reguire
candidates to file campaign statements disclosing contributions
received and expenditures rnade during specified reporting periods.
Section 84205 and Regulation 18406 pernit the Comnission-to
provide for a short forn for candidates who raise or spend less
than $1,000 in a calendar year. The Connission has deaignated
Forn 470 as the short form.


John Honigsfeld
Page Three
Section 852oo requires
Prior to solicitation or receipt of any contribution or loan,
an individual rrho intends to be a candidate for an elective
office shaIl file with the commission a statement...of
intention to be a candidate for a specific office.
After filing this statenent (Forn 501),
...the individual shall establish one canpaign contribution
account at an office of a financial institution located in
the state.
(section 85201(a) . )
Contributions may not be comningled with the personal funds
of the candidate receiving them. (Section 84307.)
Secti.on 84211(q) states that if a contribution is not cashed,
negotiated, or deposited and is returned to the contributor before
the closing date of the campaign statement on which it woutd
otherwise be reported, the contribution is deerned not to have been
accepted.
Since Section 85201(a) contains no requirernent that a
candidate's contributions be placed in a checking account, any
type of account at a california financial institution may be used
to deposit contributions. Please note, however, that Section
85201(e) requires that all of a candidaters campaign expenditures
be made frorn this account. Any expenditure from the account of
$100 or Eore cannot be in cash. (Section 84300(b).) Therefore, a
candidate nust draw funds from the account for these types of
expenditures in some forn of check or negotiable instrurnent.
If you have additional .questions, please contact ne at (916)
322-5662 .
Sincerely,
Scott Ha
Act
S. Braaten-lloen
itical Reforn Consultant
